name: Mixed Berry Tiramisu
recipeIngredient: 12 oz package unsweetened frozen mixed berries
10 oz package frozen raspberries in syrup (thawed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the frozen mixed berries and sugar in a heavy medium saucepan over medium heat until the mixture resembles jam and is reduced to 1 cup, stirring frequently for about 15 minutes. Cool the jam mixture.
2. Strain syrup from thawed raspberries through a sieve set over a bowl, pressing gently on solids. Discard solids. Add raspberry liqueur to the raspberry syrup in the bowl.
3. Trim one biscuit to a 3-inch length using a sharp knife. Dip the biscuit into the syrup, turning to coat lightly. Place the rounded end up and sugared side against the sides of a 9-inch springform pan with 2 3/4-inch sides. Repeat with additional biscuits to cover the sides of the pan. Arrange more biscuits on the bottom, covering completely and trimming to fit.
4. In a bowl, whisk mascarpone with sugar and vanilla until blended. Set aside.
5. Thinly slice enough strawberries to measure 1/2 cup. Gently spread half of the jam mixture over the biscuits in the bottom of the pan. Spoon half of the mascarpone mixture over; smooth the top. Sprinkle sliced strawberries, 1/2 cup fresh raspberries, and 1/2 cup blueberries.
6. Dip more biscuits into syrup and arrange over the fruit in the pan, covering completely and trimming to fit. Gently spread the remaining jam mixture over the biscuits. Spoon the remaining mascarpone mixture over; smooth the top.
7. Cover and chill for at least 4 hours or overnight. Release the pan sides. Transfer the cake to a platter. Arrange any remaining fresh berries decoratively atop the cake and serve.
Masc
